title: Equivalent whole number and fraction multiplication expressions
description: Practice seeing how one whole-number-times-fraction problem is the same as another.  Find equivalent multiplication expressions.
topic_path: Equivalent whole number and fraction multiplication expressions

assessment_items.question_content:
Priya is making a table with expressions that are equal to $6\times\dfrac25$.

**Help Priya complete the table.**

 | |
:- | :- |
$6\times\dfrac25$ | $= $ | $12\times\dfrac15$  
$6\times\dfrac25$| $ = $ | $4\times\dfrac35$    
$6\times\dfrac25$ | $=$ | $2\times{}$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
Priya was able to find several equivalent expressions to $6\times\dfrac25$. 

Let's see how she did it!

##Why does $6\times\dfrac{2}{5}= 12 \times \dfrac{1}{5}$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} 6\times\blueD{\dfrac25}\\\\
&= 6 \times \left({\blueD2\times \blueD{\dfrac{1}{5}}}\right)\\\\
&= (6 \times\blueD2) {\times \blueD{\dfrac{1}{5}}}\\\\
&= 12 \times \dfrac{1}{5}
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $6\times\dfrac{2}{5}$ and $12 \times \dfrac{1}{5}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##Why does $6\times\dfrac{2}{5}= 4 \times \dfrac{3}{5}$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} 6\times{\dfrac25}\\\\
&= \maroonD{12} \times \dfrac{1}{5}\\\\
&=(\maroonD4\times\maroonD3) \times \dfrac15\\\\
&=\maroonD4 \times \left( \maroonD3\times\dfrac15 \right)\\\\
&=\maroonD4 \times \dfrac{\maroonD3}5
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:



[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $6\times\dfrac{2}{5}$ and $4 \times \dfrac{3}{5}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##What completes $6\times\dfrac{2}{5}= 2 \times \square$ ?

Now, we need to find the missing information for the equivalent expression $2\times\square{}$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} \purpleE6\times{\dfrac25}\\\\
&=(\purpleE2\times\purpleE3) \times {\dfrac{2}{5}}\\\\
&=\purpleE2 \times \left(\purpleE3\times{\dfrac{2}{5}}\right)\\\\
&= 2 \times \dfrac{6}{5}
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:

[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $6\times\dfrac{2}{5}$ and $2 \times \dfrac{6}{5}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

Here is the complete table:

 | |
:- | :- |
$6\times\dfrac25$ | $= $ | $12\times\dfrac15$  
$6\times\dfrac25$| $ = $ | $4\times\dfrac35$    
$6\times\dfrac25$ | $=$ | $2\times\purpleE{\dfrac65}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Nancy is making a table with expressions that are equal to $8\times\dfrac34$.

**Help Nancy complete the table.**

 | |
:- | :- |
$8\times\dfrac34$ | $= $ | $24\times{}$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
$8\times\dfrac34$| $ = $ | $4\times\dfrac64$    
$8\times\dfrac34$ | $=$ | $6\times\dfrac44$
hints:
Nancy was able to find several equivalent expressions to $8\times\dfrac34$. 

Let's see how she did it!

##Why does $8\times\dfrac{3}{4}= 4 \times \dfrac{6}{4}$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD8\times{\dfrac34}\\\\
&=(\blueD4\times\blueD2)\times\dfrac34\\\\
&=\blueD4\times \left(\blueD2\times\dfrac34 \right)\\\\
&= \blueD4 \times \dfrac{\blueD6}{4}
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $8\times\dfrac{3}{4}$ and $4 \times \dfrac{6}{4}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##Why does $8\times\dfrac{3}{4}= 6\times\dfrac44$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} \maroonD8\times{\dfrac34}\\\\
&=( \maroonD{4\times2})\times\dfrac34\\\\
&=(\maroonD2\times3)\times \dfrac{\maroonD4}4\\\\
&=6\times\dfrac{4}{4}
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:

[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $8\times\dfrac{3}{4}$ and $ 6\times\dfrac44$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##What completes $8\times\dfrac{3}{4}= 24 \times \square$ ?

Now, we need to find the missing information for the equivalent expression $24\times\square{}$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} 8\times\dfrac3{4}\\\\
&=8\times \left( \dfrac{\redE{3}\times\redE1}{4} \right)\\\\
&=(8\times\redE3)\times\dfrac{\redE1}{4} \\\\
&= {24}\times\redE{\dfrac{1}{4}} 
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:

[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $8\times\dfrac{3}{4}$ and $24 \times \dfrac{1}{4}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

Here is the complete table:

 | |
:- | :- |
$8\times\dfrac34$ | $= $ | $24\times\redE{\dfrac14}$
$8\times\dfrac34$| $ = $ | $4\times\dfrac64$    
$8\times\dfrac34$ | $=$ | $6\times\dfrac44$

assessment_items.question_content:
Logan is making a table with expressions that are equal to $20\times\dfrac15$.

**Help Logan complete the table.**

 | |
:- | :- |
$20\times\dfrac15$ | $= $ | $2\times\dfrac{10}5$
$20\times\dfrac15$| $ = $ | $\dfrac55\times4$   
$20\times\dfrac15$ | $=$ |  [[☃ numeric-input 4]] ${}\times\dfrac25$
hints:
Logan was able to find several equivalent expressions to $20\times\dfrac15$. 

Let's see how he did it!

##Why does $20\times\dfrac15= 2\times\dfrac{10}5$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{20}\times{\dfrac15}\\\\
&=(\blueD2\times\blueD{10})\times\dfrac15\\\\
&=\blueD2\times \left(\blueD{10}\times\dfrac15 \right)\\\\
&= \blueD2 \times \dfrac{\blueD{10}}{5}
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $20\times\dfrac{1}{5}$ and $2 \times \dfrac{10}{5}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##Why does $20\times\dfrac15=\dfrac55\times4$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} \maroonD{20}\times{\dfrac15}\\\\
&= (\maroonD4\times\maroonD5)\times\dfrac15\\\\
&=\maroonD4\times \left( \maroonD5\times\dfrac15 \right)\\\\
&=\maroonD{4}\times\maroonD{\dfrac55}\\\\
&=\maroonD{\dfrac55}\times\maroonD{4}
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:

[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $20\times\dfrac{1}{5}$ and $ \dfrac55\times4$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##What completes $20\times\dfrac15= \square\times\dfrac25$ ?

Now, we need to find the missing information for the equivalent expression $\square\times\dfrac25$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} 20\times\dfrac15\\\\
&=(\redE{10}\times\redE2) \times {\dfrac{1}{5}}\\\\
&=\redE{10} \times \left( \redE2 \times{\dfrac{1}{5}} \right)\\\\
&= \redE{10} \times \redE{\dfrac{2}{5}}
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $20\times\dfrac{1}{5}$ and $10\times \dfrac{2}{5}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

Here is the complete table:

 | |
:- | :- |
$20\times\dfrac15$ | $= $ | $2\times\dfrac{10}5$
$20\times\dfrac15$| $ = $ | $\dfrac55\times4$   
$20\times\dfrac15$ | $=$ | $\redE{10}\times\dfrac25$

assessment_items.question_content:
Scott is making a table with expressions that are equal to $\dfrac4{10}\times4$.

**Help Scott complete the table.**

 | |
:- | :- |
$\dfrac4{10}\times4$ | $= $ | $2\times\dfrac{8}{10}$
$\dfrac4{10}\times4$| $ = $ | [[☃ numeric-input 5]] $\times\dfrac1{10}$
$\dfrac4{10}\times4$ | $=$ |  $\dfrac2{10}\times8$
hints:
Scott was able to find several equivalent expressions to $\dfrac4{10}\times4$. 

Let's see how he did it!

##Why does $\dfrac4{10}\times4=2\times\dfrac{8}{10}$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\dfrac4{10}\times\blueD4\\\\
&=\dfrac4{10}\times(\blueD2\times\blueD2)\\\\
&=\left(\dfrac4{10}\times\blueD2\right)\times\blueD2\\\\
&= \dfrac{\blueD{8}}{10}\times\blueD2\\\\
&=2\dfrac{8}{10}
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:



[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac{4}{10}\times4$ and $2\dfrac{8}{10}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##Why does $\dfrac4{10}\times4=\dfrac2{10}\times8$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} {\maroonD{\dfrac4{10}}}\times4\\\\
&=\dfrac{\maroonD2\times\maroonD2}{10}\times4\\\\
&=\dfrac2{10}\times(\maroonD2\times4)\\\\
&=\dfrac2{10}\times\maroonD8
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac{4}{10}\times4$ and $ \dfrac2{10}\times8$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##What completes $\dfrac4{10}\times4= \square\times\dfrac1{10}$ ?

Now, we need to find the missing information for the equivalent expression $\square\times\dfrac1{10}$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} \dfrac4{10}\times4\\\\
&=\left(\dfrac{\goldD{1}\times\goldD4}{10}\right) \times 4\\\\
&=\dfrac{\goldD1}{10} \times (\goldD4\times4)\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{10} \times \goldD{16}
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac{4}{10}\times4$ and $\dfrac{1}{10}\times16$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

Here is the complete table:

 | |
:- | :- |
$\dfrac4{10}\times4$ | $= $ | $2\times\dfrac{8}{10}$
$\dfrac4{10}\times4$| $ = $ | $\goldD{16}\times\dfrac1{10}$
$\dfrac4{10}\times4$ | $=$ |  $\dfrac2{10}\times8$

assessment_items.question_content:
Richard is making a table with expressions that are equal to $\dfrac43\times5$.

**Help Richard complete the table.**

 | |
:- | :- |
$\dfrac43\times5$ | $= $ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]] ${}\times\dfrac{1}{3}$
$\dfrac43\times5$ | $ = $ | $10\times\dfrac23$
$\dfrac43\times5$| $=$ |  $\dfrac{10}{3}\times2$
hints:
Richard was able to find several equivalent expressions to $\dfrac43\times5$. 

Let's see how he did it!

##Why does $\dfrac43\times5=10\times\dfrac23$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{\dfrac43}\times5\\\\
&=\left(\dfrac{\blueD2\times\blueD2}{\blueD3} \right) \times5\\\\
&=\dfrac23\times(\blueD2\times5)\\\\
&=\dfrac23\times10\\\\
&=10\times\dfrac23
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac43\times5$ and $10\times\dfrac23$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##Why does $\dfrac43\times5=\dfrac{10}{3}\times2$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} {\maroonD{\dfrac4{3}}}\times5\\\\
&=\dfrac{\maroonD2\times\maroonD2}{3}\times5\\\\
&=\maroonD2\times \left(\dfrac{\maroonD2\times5}{3}\right)\\\\
&=2\times\dfrac{10}3\\\\
&=\dfrac{10}{3}\times2
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac43\times5$ and $\dfrac{10}{3}\times2$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##What completes $\dfrac43\times5= \square\times\dfrac13$ ?

Now, we need to find the missing information for the equivalent expression $\square\times\dfrac13$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} {\redE{\dfrac4{3}}}\times5\\\\
&=5\times\dfrac{\redE4\times\redE1}{3}\\\\
&=5\times\redE4\times\dfrac{\redE1}{3}\\\\
&=\redE{20}\times\dfrac13
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:

[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac43\times5$ and $20\times\dfrac{1}{3}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

Here is the complete table:

 | |
:- | :- |
$\dfrac43\times5$ | $= $ | $\redE{20}\times\dfrac{1}{3}$
$\dfrac43\times5$ | $ = $ | $10\times\dfrac23$
$\dfrac43\times5$| $=$ |  $\dfrac{10}{3}\times2$

assessment_items.question_content:
Emily is making a table with expressions that are equal to $\dfrac32\times2$.

**Help Emily complete the table.**

 | |
:- | :- |
$\dfrac32\times2$ | $= $ | $\dfrac{2}{2}\times3$
$\dfrac32\times2$ | $ = $ | $6\times{}$ [[☃ numeric-input 6]] 
$\dfrac32\times2$| $=$ |  $1\times\dfrac62$
hints:
Emily was able to find several equivalent expressions to $\dfrac32\times2$. 

Let's see how she did it!

##Why does $\dfrac32\times2=\dfrac{2}{2}\times3$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{\dfrac32}\times2\\\\
&=\left(\dfrac{\blueD3\times\blueD1}{\blueD2}\right) \times 2\\\\
&=\blueD3\times \left(\dfrac{\blueD1\times2}{2} \right)\\\\
&=\blueD3\times\dfrac22\\\\
&=\dfrac22\times3
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac32\times2$ and $3\times\dfrac{2}{2}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##Why does $\dfrac32\times2=1\times\dfrac62$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} {\maroonD{\dfrac3{2}}}\times2\\\\
&=\dfrac{\maroonD1\times\maroonD3}{2}\times2\\\\
&=\maroonD1\times \left(\dfrac{\maroonD3\times2}{2} \right)\\\\
&=1\times\dfrac62
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:



[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac32\times2$ and $1\times\dfrac62$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##What completes $\dfrac32\times2= \square\times\dfrac1{2}$ ?

Now, we need to find the missing information for the equivalent expression $\square\times\dfrac1{2}$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} {\goldD{\dfrac3{2}}}\times2\\\\
&=\dfrac{\goldD1\times\goldD3}{2}\times2\\\\
&=\goldD{\dfrac1{2}}\times(3\times2)\\\\
&=\goldD{\dfrac1{2}}\times6
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac{3}{2}\times2$ and $\dfrac{1}{2}\times6$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

Here is the complete table:

 | |
:- | :- |
$\dfrac32\times2$ | $= $ | $\dfrac{2}{2}\times3$
$\dfrac32\times2$ | $ = $ | $6\times{}\goldD{\dfrac12}$
$\dfrac32\times2$| $=$ |  $1\times\dfrac62$

assessment_items.question_content:
Jackson is making a table with expressions that are equal to $\dfrac9{10}\times3$.

**Help Jackson complete the table.**

 | |
:- | :- |
$\dfrac9{10}\times3$ | $= $ | $27\times{}$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
$\dfrac9{10}\times3$ | $ = $ | $9\times\dfrac3{10}$
$\dfrac9{10}\times3$| $=$ |  $\dfrac{27}{10}\times1$
hints:
Jackson was able to find several equivalent expressions to $\dfrac9{10}\times3$. 

Let's see how he did it!

##Why does $\dfrac9{10}\times3=9\times\dfrac3{10}$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{\dfrac9{10}}\times3\\\\
&=\left(\dfrac{\blueD3\times\blueD3}{\blueD{10}} \right) \times3\\\\
&=\blueD{\dfrac3{10}}\times(3\times3)\\\\
&=\dfrac3{10}\times9\\\\
&=9\times\dfrac3{10}
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:

[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac9{10}\times3$ and $9\times\dfrac3{10}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##Why does $\dfrac9{10}\times3=\dfrac{27}{10}\times1$ ?

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} {\maroonD{\dfrac4{3}}}\times5\\\\
&=\dfrac{\maroonD2\times\maroonD2}{3}\times5\\\\
&=\maroonD2\times \left( \dfrac{\maroonD2\times5}{3} \right)\\\\
&=2\times\dfrac{10}3\\\\
&=\dfrac{10}{3}\times2
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac9{10}\times3$ and $\dfrac{27}{10}\times1$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

##What completes $\dfrac9{10}\times3= 27\times\square$ ?

Now, we need to find the missing information for the equivalent expression $27\times\square$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=} \dfrac9{10}\times3\\\\
&=\dfrac{\redE1\times\redE9}{10}\times3\\\\
&=\dfrac1{10}\times(\redE9\times3)\\\\
&=\dfrac1{10}\times\redE{27}
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:

[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac9{10}\times3$ and $27\times\dfrac{1}{10}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

Here is the complete table:

 | |
:- | :- |
$\dfrac9{10}\times3$ | $= $ | $27\times\redE{\dfrac1{10}}$
$\dfrac9{10}\times3$ | $ = $ | $9\times\dfrac3{10}$
$\dfrac9{10}\times3$| $=$ |  $\dfrac{27}{10}\times1$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select two expressions that are equivalent to $4 \times \dfrac{3}{10}$.**


[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
Let's see how we can rearrange the expression $4\times\dfrac3{10}$ to find other equivalent expressions.

Remember, we can break apart $\dfrac{3}{10}$ into a unit fraction and rearrange the numbers to make equivalent expressions.

$\begin{align}
4 \times \blueD{\dfrac{3}{10}} &=4 \times \left(\blueD{3\times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right)\\\\
&=(4 \times \blueD3) \blueD{\times \dfrac{1}{10}}\\\\
&= 12 \times \dfrac{1}{10}
\end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $4\times\dfrac{3}{10}$ and $12 \times \dfrac{1}{10}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

$\begin{align}
\greenD4 \times {\dfrac{3}{10}} &= (\greenD2\times\greenD2) \times \dfrac{3}{10}\\\\
&=\greenD2\times \left(\greenD2 \times\dfrac{3}{10}\right)\\\\
&= 2 \times \dfrac{6}{10}\\\\
&=\dfrac{6}{10}\times2
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $4\times\dfrac{3}{10}$ and $2 \times \dfrac{6}{10}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

The following expressions are equivalent to $4 \times \dfrac{3}{10}$:

* $\dfrac6{10}\times 2$

* $3\times\dfrac{4}{10}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select two expressions that are equivalent to $3 \times \dfrac{7}{4}$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's see how we can rearrange the expression $3\times\dfrac74$ to find other equivalent expressions.


Remember, we can break apart $\dfrac{7}{4}$ into a unit fraction and rearrange the numbers to make equivalent expressions.

$\begin{align}
3 \times \blueD{\dfrac{7}{4}} &=3 \times \left(\blueD{7\times \dfrac{1}{4}}\right)\\\\
&=(3 \times \blueD7) \blueD{\times \dfrac{1}{4}}\\\\
&= 21 \times \dfrac{1}{4}
\end{align}$

[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $3\times\dfrac{7}{4}$ and $21 \times \dfrac{1}{4}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

$\begin{align}
\greenD3 \times {\dfrac{7}{4}} &= (\greenD3\times7) \times \dfrac{1}{4}\\\\
&=7\times \left(\greenD3 \times\dfrac{1}{4}\right)\\\\
&= 7 \times \dfrac{3}{4}\\\\
&=\dfrac{3}{4}\times7
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:

[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $3\times\dfrac{7}{4}$ and $\dfrac{3}{4}\times7$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

The following expressions are equivalent to $3 \times \dfrac{7}{4}$:

* $\dfrac3{4}\times 7$

* $21\times\dfrac{1}{4}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select two expressions that are equivalent to $2 \times \dfrac{10}{3}$. **


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's see how we can rearrange the expression $2\times\dfrac{10}3$ to find other equivalent expressions.


Remember, we can break apart $\dfrac{10}{3}$ into a unit fraction and rearrange the numbers to make equivalent expressions.

$\begin{align}
2 \times \blueD{\dfrac{10}{3}} &=2 \times \left(\blueD{10\times \dfrac{1}{3}}\right)\\\\
&=(2 \times \blueD{10}) \blueD{\times \dfrac{1}{3}}\\\\
&= 20 \times \dfrac{1}{3}
\end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $2\times\dfrac{10}{3}$ and $20 \times \dfrac{1}{3}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

$\begin{align}
2\times {\greenD{\dfrac{10}{3}}} &= 2\times \dfrac{\greenD2\times\greenD5}{3}\\\\
&=(2\times \greenD2) \times\dfrac{\greenD5}{3}\\\\
&= 4 \times \dfrac{5}{3}\\\\
&=\dfrac{5}{3}\times4
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $2\times\dfrac{10}{3}$ and $\dfrac{5}{3}\times4$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

The following expressions are equivalent to $2\times \dfrac{10}{3}$:

* $\dfrac53\times 4$

* $2\times\dfrac{10}{3}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select two expressions that are equivalent to $\dfrac32 \times 4$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's see how we can rearrange the expression $\dfrac32\times4$ to find other equivalent expressions.


Remember, we can break apart $\dfrac{3}{2}$ into a unit fraction and rearrange the numbers to make equivalent expressions.

$\begin{align}
\blueD{\dfrac{3}{2}}\times4 &=\left(\blueD{\dfrac{1}{2}}\times\blueD3\right)\times4\\\\
&=\dfrac12\times(\blueD3 \times4)\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2}\times12\\\\
&=12\times\dfrac12
\end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac{3}{2}\times4$ and $\dfrac12 \times 12$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac32\times \greenD4&= \dfrac32 \times (\greenD2\times\greenD2)\\\\
&=\left(\dfrac32\times \greenD2\right) \times\greenD2\\\\
&= \dfrac{6}{2}\times2
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:



[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac{3}{2}\times4$ and $\dfrac{6}{2}\times2$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

The following expressions are equivalent to $\dfrac{3}{2}\times4$:

* $12\times\dfrac12$

* $\dfrac{6}{2}\times2$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select two expressions that are equivalent to $\dfrac3{10}\times8$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's see how we can rearrange the expression $\dfrac3{10}\times8$ to find other equivalent expressions.


Remember, we can break apart $\dfrac{3}{10}$ into a unit fraction and rearrange the numbers to make equivalent expressions.

$\begin{align}
{\dfrac{3}{10}}\times\blueD8 &={\dfrac{3}{10}}\times(\blueD4\times\blueD2)\\\\
&=(\dfrac1{10}\times3)\times(\blueD4 \times\blueD2)\\\\
&= (\blueD4\times3)\times (\dfrac{1}{10}\times\blueD2)\\\\
&=12\times\dfrac2{10}
\end{align}$



[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac{3}{10}\times8$ and $12\dfrac2{10}$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac3{10}\times \greenD8&=\left(\greenD{\dfrac1{10}\times
3}\right)\times 8\\\\
&=\greenD{\dfrac1{10}}\times(\greenD3\times8)\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{10}\times24
\end{align}$

We can also visualize the expressions:

[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac{3}{10}\times8$ and $\dfrac{1}{10}\times24$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

The following expressions are equivalent to $\dfrac{3}{10}\times8$:

* $12\dfrac2{10}$

* $\dfrac{1}{10}\times24$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select two expressions that are equivalent to $\dfrac1{3}\times4$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's see how we can rearrange the expression $\dfrac1{3}\times4$ to find other equivalent expressions.

$\begin{align}
{\dfrac{1}{3}}\times\blueD4 &={\dfrac{1}{3}}\times(\blueD2\times\blueD2)\\\\
&=(\dfrac1{3}\times\blueD2)\times\blueD2\\\\
&= \dfrac{\blueD2}{3} \times \blueD2\\\\
&=2\times\dfrac23
\end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac{1}{3}\times4$ and $2\times\dfrac23$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

$\begin{align}
{\dfrac{1}{3}}\times\greenD4 &={\dfrac{1}{3}}\times(\greenD4\times\greenD1)\\\\
&=\left(\dfrac1{3}\times\greenD4\right)\times\greenD1\\\\
&=\dfrac43\times1
\end{align}$



[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $\dfrac{1}{3}\times4$ and $\dfrac4{3}\times1$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

The following expressions are equivalent to $\dfrac{1}{3}\times4$:

* $2\times\dfrac23$

* $\dfrac4{3}\times1$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select two expressions that are equivalent to $\dfrac15 \times 24$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's see how we can rearrange the expression $\dfrac15 \times 24$ to find other equivalent expressions.

$\begin{align}
{\blueD{24}\times\dfrac{1}{5}}&=(\blueD6\times\blueD4)\times\dfrac15\\\\
&=\blueD6\times(\blueD4\times\dfrac15)\\\\
&= \blueD6\times\dfrac{\blueD4}5
\end{align}$

[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $24\times\dfrac{1}{5}$ and $6\times\dfrac45$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

$\begin{align}
\greenD{24}\times\dfrac15&=(\greenD8\times\greenD3)\times\dfrac15\\\\
&=\greenD8\times \left(\greenD3\times\dfrac15\right)\\\\
&=\greenD8\times\dfrac{\greenD3}5
\end{align}$


[[☃ image 1]]

The expressions $24\times\dfrac{1}{5}$ and $\dfrac35\times8$ are on the same point on the number line, so they are equivalent.

The following expressions are equivalent to $24\times\dfrac{1}{5}$:

* $6\times\dfrac45$

* $\dfrac35\times8$
